---
title: "Theming"
subtitle: "Map every visible region of a plot to the `theme()` key that styles it."
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
engine: markdown
---

A _Gribouille_ plot is a stack of named regions: an outer canvas, an inner panel (or grid of panels), a strip band for facets, a legend column, a title block on top, a caption underneath, and axes around the edges.
Each region is one entry in the [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) dictionary, set with an [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd), [`element-line`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd), [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd), [`element-blank`](reference/themes/element-blank.qmd), or [`margin`](reference/themes/margin.qmd) value.
This page is a map: it shows where each region sits on a real plot, names the [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) key that controls it, and links worked examples for the common overrides.

## Anatomy of a plot

The diagram below is a real plot.
Each region carries a deliberate fill so you can see where it sits, and a callout names the [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) key that styles it.
Compose your own overrides by replacing any of those keys.

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-theme-anatomy
//| output-filename: "/assets/typst-render/theming/theme-anatomy.svg"
//| alt: "A faceted scatter plot annotated with callouts naming every styled region: plot-background and plot-margin around the outside, plot-title and plot-subtitle on top, plot-caption at the bottom, panel-background and panel-grid inside each facet, strip-background and strip-text on the facet header band, axis-line, axis-ticks, axis-text, and axis-title on each side, and legend-background, legend-title, and legend-text on the right."
#import "@preview/cetz:{{< var dependencies.cetz.version >}}": canvas, draw

#let ink = if _typst_render_foreground != none {
  _typst_render_foreground
} else {
  rgb("#1f2328")
}
#let muted = rgb("#666666")

#let demo-data = (
  (g: "A", x: 1, y: 2.4),
  (g: "A", x: 2, y: 3.1),
  (g: "A", x: 3, y: 3.8),
  (g: "A", x: 4, y: 4.2),
  (g: "B", x: 1, y: 1.6),
  (g: "B", x: 2, y: 2.0),
  (g: "B", x: 3, y: 2.7),
  (g: "B", x: 4, y: 3.1),
)

#let demo-plot = plot(
  data: demo-data,
  mapping: aes(x: "x", y: "y", colour: "g"),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 3pt),),
  facet: facet-wrap("g"),
  labs: labs(
    title: "Anatomy of a plot",
    subtitle: "Each region is one theme() key",
    caption: "Caption text lives in plot-caption",
    x: "x (axis-title)",
    y: "y (axis-title)",
    colour: "Group",
  ),
  theme: theme(
    plot-background: element-rect(fill: rgb("#fffaf0")),
    panel-background: element-rect(fill: rgb("#eef4ff")),
    strip-background: element-rect(fill: rgb("#ffe2d6")),
    legend-background: element-rect(fill: rgb("#e6f4ea"), stroke: 0.3pt + rgb("#bcd9c4")),
    panel-grid: element-line(colour: rgb("#c0c8d4"), stroke: 0.4pt),
    axis-line: element-line(stroke: 0.6pt),
    axis-ticks: element-line(stroke: 0.6pt),
    plot-margin: margin(top: 0.4cm, right: 0.4cm, bottom: 0.4cm, left: 0.4cm),
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 6.5cm,
)

#canvas(length: 1cm, {
  import draw: *
  set-style(content: (frame: none))

  let px = 4.5
  let py = 4.0
  let pw = 12.0
  let ph = 6.5

  content(
    (px, py),
    anchor: "south-west",
    demo-plot,
  )

  let cstroke = 0.5pt + muted

  let callout(from, label-pos, label-anchor, body) = {
    line(label-pos, from, stroke: cstroke, mark: (end: ">"))
    content(label-pos, anchor: label-anchor, body)
  }

  let lab(name, keys) = text(size: 7.5pt, fill: ink)[
    *#name* \
    #text(size: 7pt, font: "DejaVu Sans Mono", fill: muted)[#keys]
  ]

  callout(
    (px + 0.05, py + ph - 0.1),
    (px - 0.6, py + ph + 1.6),
    "south-east",
    lab("Outer canvas", [`plot-background`]),
  )

  callout(
    (px + 0.55, py + ph - 0.55),
    (px - 0.6, py + ph + 0.4),
    "south-east",
    lab("Inset around content", [`plot-margin`]),
  )

  callout(
    (px + 0.9, py + ph * 0.5),
    (px - 0.6, py + ph * 0.55 - 0.4),
    "south-east",
    lab(
      "Left axis",
      [
        `axis-line-y-left` \
        `axis-ticks-y-left` \
        `axis-text-y-left` \
        `axis-title-y-left`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + pw * 0.35, py + 0.6),
    (px - 0.6, py - 0.3),
    "south-east",
    lab(
      "Bottom axis",
      [
        `axis-line-x-bottom` \
        `axis-ticks-x-bottom` \
        `axis-text-x-bottom` \
        `axis-title-x-bottom`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + 2.0, py + ph - 0.5),
    (px + pw + 0.5, py + ph + 1.4),
    "south-west",
    lab(
      "Title block",
      [
        `plot-title` \
        `plot-subtitle`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + pw * 0.45, py + ph - 1.3),
    (px + pw + 0.5, py + ph + 0.2),
    "south-west",
    lab(
      "Facet strip",
      [
        `strip-background` \
        `strip-text`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + pw * 0.55, py + ph * 0.55),
    (px + pw + 0.5, py + ph * 0.55 + 0.4),
    "south-west",
    lab(
      "Panel",
      [
        `panel-background` \
        `panel-grid`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + pw - 0.6, py + ph * 0.5),
    (px + pw + 0.5, py + ph * 0.5 - 1.6),
    "south-west",
    lab(
      "Legend",
      [
        `legend-background` \
        `legend-title` \
        `legend-text` \
        `legend-bar` \
        `legend-ticks`
      ],
    ),
  )

  callout(
    (px + pw * 0.5, py + 0.35),
    (px + pw + 0.5, py - 0.5),
    "south-west",
    lab("Caption", [`plot-caption`]),
  )
})
```

## Region to theme key reference

The table below names every region the renderer styles and the [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) key that controls it.
Per-side axis keys (`-x-bottom`, `-x-top`, `-y-left`, `-y-right`) cascade up to the unprefixed family, so setting `axis-text` alone styles all four sides at once.

| Region | Key(s) | Element type |
|---|---|---|
| Outer plot rectangle. | `plot-background`. | [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd). |
| Inset between the outer rectangle and the content. | `plot-margin`. | [`margin`](reference/themes/margin.qmd). |
| Title, subtitle, caption. | `plot-title`, `plot-subtitle`, `plot-caption`. | [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd). |
| Drawing area inside a facet. | `panel-background`. | [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd). |
| Gridlines inside the panel. | `panel-grid`. | [`element-line`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd). |
| Facet header band. | `strip-background`, `strip-text`. | [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd), [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd). |
| Axis line, per side. | `axis-line`, `axis-line-x-bottom`, `axis-line-x-top`, `axis-line-y-left`, `axis-line-y-right`. | [`element-line`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd). |
| Axis ticks, per side. | `axis-ticks`, `axis-ticks-x-bottom`, `axis-ticks-x-top`, `axis-ticks-y-left`, `axis-ticks-y-right`. | [`element-line`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd). |
| Tick mark length, per side. | `tick-length`, `tick-length-x-bottom`, `tick-length-x-top`, `tick-length-y-left`, `tick-length-y-right`. | Typst length. |
| Axis tick labels, per side. | `axis-text`, `axis-text-x-bottom`, `axis-text-x-top`, `axis-text-y-left`, `axis-text-y-right`. | [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd). |
| Axis title, per side. | `axis-title`, `axis-title-x-bottom`, `axis-title-x-top`, `axis-title-y-left`, `axis-title-y-right`. | [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd). |
| Discrete legend box. | `legend-background`, `legend-title`, `legend-text`. | [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd), [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd). |
| Continuous colour bar. | `legend-bar`, `legend-ticks`. | [`element-rect`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd), [`element-line`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd). |
| Shared cascade parents. | `text`, `line`, `rect`. | Element constructors. |
| Layer aesthetic defaults. | `geom`. | [`element-geom`](reference/themes/element-geom.qmd). |

Any key can be hidden with [`element-blank`](reference/themes/element-blank.qmd) in place of its normal element.
That is how [`theme-minimal`](reference/themes/theme-minimal.qmd) drops the axis line and the panel background, for example.

## Override one region

The lightest possible change is to pass a single record into [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) for the region you care about.
The example below lifts the [`axis-title`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd) and [`axis-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd) sizes, paints the [`panel-background`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd) cream, and softens the [`panel-grid`](reference/themes/element-line.qmd).
Every other surface keeps its default.

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "/assets/typst-render/theming/override.svg"
//| alt: "Scatter plot with cream panel background, soft gridlines, and enlarged axis titles."
#let df = range(0, 12).map(i => (x: i, y: i * i * 0.1))

#plot(
  data: df,
  mapping: aes(x: "x", y: "y"),
  layers: (
    geom-line(stroke: 1.2pt, colour: rgb("#d6604d")),
    geom-point(size: 3pt, fill: rgb("#d6604d")),
  ),
  theme: theme(
    axis-title: element-text(size: 12pt),
    axis-text: element-text(size: 10pt),
    panel-background: element-rect(fill: rgb("#f7f0e7")),
    panel-grid: element-line(colour: rgb("#d9cfbf")),
  ),
  labs: labs(
    title: "theme() overrides",
    subtitle: "Larger axis titles, a cream panel fill, and a soft grid",
    x: "Step",
    y: "y = 0.1 × x²",
  ),
  width: 12cm,
  height: 7cm,
)
```

Each line maps directly to one row in the [region table](#region-to-theme-key-reference): change a single key, the matching region updates, nothing else moves.

## Tune the outer canvas

The gap between the [`plot-background`](reference/themes/element-rect.qmd) rectangle and the title block is [`plot-margin`](reference/themes/margin.qmd).
Pass [`margin`](reference/themes/margin.qmd) with any subset of `top`, `right`, `bottom`, `left`; sides left at `auto` fall through to the renderer's defaults.

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| output-filename: "/assets/typst-render/theming/margin.svg"
//| alt: "Two stacked scatter plots; the lower one has wider left and bottom plot-margins than the upper one."
#let accent = rgb("#1f77b4")
#let d = range(0, 10).map(i => (x: i, y: i * 0.5))

#let panel(title, theme-arg) = plot(
  data: d,
  mapping: aes(x: "x", y: "y"),
  layers: (
    geom-line(stroke: 1pt, colour: accent),
    geom-point(size: 2pt, fill: accent),
  ),
  labs: labs(title: title, x: "X", y: "Y"),
  theme: theme-arg,
  width: 12cm,
  height: 6cm,
)

#grid(
  columns: 1,
  row-gutter: 0.4cm,
  panel("Default plot-margin", theme-minimal()),
  panel(
    "margin(top: 0.6cm, right: 0.6cm, bottom: 0.9cm, left: 1.6cm)",
    theme-minimal(plot-margin: margin(
      top: 0.6cm,
      right: 0.6cm,
      bottom: 0.9cm,
      left: 1.6cm,
    )),
  ),
)
```

`plot-margin` shifts only the outer inset; column gutters between facet panels and the gap between the panel and the axis labels are controlled by the surrounding `axis-title.margin` and per-element `margin` fields on each [`element-text`](reference/themes/element-text.qmd).

## Complete vs partial themes

[`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) is the full base: every surface listed in the [reference table](#region-to-theme-key-reference) is populated.
Use it when you want to start from scratch and define each region yourself.

The library ships eight curated overrides on top of that base.
Each one is a thin function that returns a theme with a handful of fields replaced; everything else cascades from [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd).

| Function | Effect |
|---|---|
| [`theme-minimal`](reference/themes/theme-minimal.qmd). | Drops the axis line, the panel background, and the tick marks. The library default. |
| [`theme-bw`](reference/themes/theme-bw.qmd). | Black-and-white frame, white panel, light grid. |
| [`theme-classic`](reference/themes/theme-classic.qmd). | Axis lines only, no grid, no panel background. |
| [`theme-grey`](reference/themes/theme-grey.qmd). | Grey panel background with a white grid. |
| [`theme-light`](reference/themes/theme-light.qmd). | Light frame and grid on a white panel. |
| [`theme-dark`](reference/themes/theme-dark.qmd). | Dark frame and grid on a dark panel. |
| [`theme-linedraw`](reference/themes/theme-linedraw.qmd). | Black frame, dark grid, white panel. |
| [`theme-void`](reference/themes/theme-void.qmd). | Everything removable removed; useful for inset thumbnails. |

The `theme-sub-*` helpers go one level finer: they bundle the keys that move together for a single region.
For example, [`theme-sub-axis`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis.qmd) accepts `line`, `ticks`, `text`, `title`, and rewrites the four corresponding [`theme()`](reference/themes/theme.qmd) keys for you.
The full set is [`theme-sub-axis`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-x`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-x.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-y`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-y.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-bottom`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-bottom.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-top`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-top.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-left`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-left.qmd), [`theme-sub-axis-right`](reference/themes/theme-sub-axis-right.qmd), [`theme-sub-legend`](reference/themes/theme-sub-legend.qmd), [`theme-sub-panel`](reference/themes/theme-sub-panel.qmd), [`theme-sub-plot`](reference/themes/theme-sub-plot.qmd), and [`theme-sub-strip`](reference/themes/theme-sub-strip.qmd).

Finally, [`theme-set`](reference/themes/theme-set.qmd) installs a theme as the document-wide default so subsequent `plot()` calls pick it up without an explicit `theme:` argument.

## Where to go next

- Browse the [themes gallery](examples/index.qmd#themes-gallery) for side-by-side renders of every built-in theme, alongside the other [theme examples](examples/index.qmd#themes).
- See [`theme-sub-*` in action](examples/index.qmd#theme-sub) for compact ways to retouch one region at a time.
- The full [themes reference](reference/themes/index.qmd) lists every element constructor and every cascade rule.
